MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Nov-07-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Alexis McKenna for plaintiff and moving party: Julie Vega
Thomas P. Klein for defendant and opposing party: Hollywood Video, Hollywood Entertaiment Corporation and Anthony Trivisonno

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
Plaintiff's motion to quash employment and medical record subpoenas is granted in part. The subpoena to Southwest Airlines is modified to only require production of any job descriptions and compensation or payroll records. The social security number of the plaintiff shall be redacted.

The motion to quash is granted as to the subpoena to Kaiser. As drafted, the subpoena is overbroad and defendant has not established good cause for the production of the requested records. The filing of the instant action does not by itself constitute a waiver of the right to privacy. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
